parsed_review.summary: CP00-757 (Migration Platform domain implementation, RP25.P01.M02.S09-RP25.P01.M04.S06, Risk B, 40 units, descriptor-only) is supported for its production_ready=descriptor_verified claim. DIRECTLY VERIFIED via Read/Grep/Glob: (1) registry.js CP757 requirements match the pack baseline exactly — deliverable distribution {implementation:23,ui:5,fixture:2,test:6,hermes_evidence:2,claude_review:2}=40 and micro_title counts {Type And Shape Definition:12,Primary Implementation Slice:22,Secondary Workflow Slice:6}; (2) required_section_rows slices map correctly to included units (M02 S09-S20, M03 S01-S22, M04 S01-S06); (3) validators.js enforces descriptor_only, runtime_execution=false, no sensitive/source payloads, no source-system/import runtime opened, no cross-tenant access, no permission-decision or audit-body leakage, plus gate/handoff/authority-boundary checks; (4) model.test.js asserts CP756/CP757 invariants incl. runtime-closed, read-only Claude, correct handoff, and latest contract projection; (5) the generated contracts/migration-platform-contract.json embeds validation.valid=true with empty errors arrays for both packs' coverage and descriptor blocks and matches every test assertion; (6) MIGRATION_PLATFORM_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION is comprehensive (no product-state writes, no db rows, no object storage, no external/source-system runtime); (7) import-plan.js/mappers.js keep runtime closed, dry-run/human-approval required, no cross-tenant, no payloads; (8) authority boundary holds — claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, Hermes packets emit_runtime_receipt=false/writes_product_state=false (deterministic control-plane pattern, not product runtime); (9) manifest pre-review state correct (production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, exactly_one_valid_pack_review_required=true, valid_review_receipts=[]); (10) plan live_state and next-pack-queue put CP00-757 as live latest with CP00-758 queued at RP25.P01.M04.S07; (11) all referenced npm scripts exist and root `npm test` includes packages/migration/test; (12) no stale review receipt/raw-output exists (correctly generated post-review); unrelated dirty/untracked files (incl. the LDIP invalid-attempt file and workbook docs) are correctly classified out-of-scope. REPORTED BUT NOT RE-EXECUTED (read-only, no Bash): the command-evidence.json exit codes (all 0); these were not independently re-run. The pack does not claim runtime_ready or enterprise trust, so the human/enterprise-trust authority boundary is outside this receipt. One non-blocking P3 on command-evidence output auditability; no P0/P1/P2.

findings.title: command-evidence.json records exit codes but empty stdout/stderr for all commands
findings.details: Every command entry in command-evidence.json (including npm test, npm run validate, npm run weighted:validate, node --test of the migration model tests, etc.) records exit_code:0 but stores stdout:"" and stderr:"". A grep for any non-empty stdout returned zero matches. This means the evidence file is exit-code-only: it does not preserve test pass counts, validation summary lines, or other output that would let a reviewer confirm WHAT each command reported. As a read-only reviewer with no Bash, I could not re-execute these commands, so the command-level pass evidence is reported-only. This is partially mitigated because the actual computed validation results are independently embedded in the generated contracts/migration-platform-contract.json (validation.valid=true with empty errors arrays for both packs' coverage and descriptor blocks), which I verified directly, and because all exit codes are 0. It is a weakness in auditability rather than a correctness defect.
findings.recommendation: In future command-evidence captures, persist at least summary stdout (e.g., node:test pass/fail tallies and validator 'passed' lines) so command outcomes are auditable from the evidence file itself rather than only via exit codes. No change required to close CP00-757; substantive validation is independently verifiable in the embedded contract projection.
findings.post_review_disposition: accepted_non_blocking; evidence-quality observation only; mitigated by embedded contract validation results and exit_code=0; does not block pack or goal closeout
